descrizione: The project isn't just a game. Once all the challenges are completed, the experience culminates with a visual journey to the Moon and becomes a veritable historical archive. Players are rewarded with an interface that allows them to explore original videos and NASA archive footage for each of the facilities they encounter (from David Scott's original experiment on the Moon to actual footage of the Centrifuge and the N.B.L. pool).

An interactive page designed for scientific outreach and virtual exploration of the Mars 2020 mission and NASA's Perseverance rover. The project is a digital laboratory where the user stops being a passive spectator and becomes a virtual operator, discovering every detail of the mission.
Radiowawe NASA is an interactive digital archive that collects and visually presents the ten most iconic audio transmissions in NASA history.
An interactive exploration of 11 confirmed exoplanets from the NASA Exoplanet Archive, selected to show the extreme diversity of worlds beyond our solar system.

This interactive archive explores how global media reported the Apollo 11 moon landing in July 1969. Through dynamic visualization, the site compares journalistic narratives from different countries, highlighting differences in tone, language, and perspective among international publications. The project illuminates the geopolitical context of the Cold War, contrasting the triumphant enthusiasm of Western media with the more sober, technical coverage in the Soviet bloc countries. It was created to celebrate NASA's 70th anniversary.

By combining technical visualization with detailed descriptions of five NASA Earth-observing satellites, this project examines the ongoing impact of climate change. The initiative is designed to illustrate how these satellites operate and why they are essential for monitoring our planet’s health.
The Lunar Archive Explorer is a digital platform that allows you to navigate and consult the scientific data and high-resolution images collected during missions to the Moon.

Nebula Vision offers a guided journey to discover the Eagle and Helix nebulae, key Milky Way pillars for understanding the origin and fate of our Solar System. Leveraging the Spitzer Space Telescope’s infrared technology, the site peers through cosmic dust to analyze scientific data otherwise invisible to the naked eye.

This project explores the Solar System starting from a clear critical issue. Many widely used representations do not accurately conveythe proportions between planetary sizes and the distances in space. The scale of the Solar System is indeed so immense that a faithful representation within a single image is impossible, often leading to misleading simplifications. To overcome this limitation, the project deconstructs the system into three primary elements, planetary sizes, positions and distances, creating an interactive visualization that translates scientific data into a clear and immediate visual experience, making the true scale of the Solar System tangible.

My project aims to pay tribute to the Hubble satellite. From 1985 to today, thanks to its contribution, we have obtained the first detailed images of space, which have been fundamental for the study and understanding of the universe.

Neo-Sentinel is an interactive web ecosystem designed to monitor Near-Earth Objects (NEOs) in real time. Developed to celebrate NASA's 70th anniversary, the project transforms complex astrometric data streams from the Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) into a space surveillance interface.

Beyond The Myth is a relational digital ecosystem that maps the evolution of astronomical knowledge, documenting the fundamental transition from mythological narrative to measurable scientific proof. The core of the project is a timeline of paradigms that weaves together chronology, narrative insights, and iconographic materials, serving as the backbone for a system of hyperlinks between historical figures and scientific instruments. Through the use of interactive tags, users can navigate transversally between scientists' biographies and the evolution of instrumentation, observing how tools transformed from rudimentary analog objects into complex digital technologies. This exploratory journey finds its final validation in the Observatory section, an area dedicated exclusively to real-time numerical data where dynamic calculations—such as the distance traveled by light during the browsing session or the exact time elapsed since the launch of Sputnik—transform historical suggestion into a tangible and objective scientific reality.

Spacesuit Evolution è un progetto che racconta l’evoluzione delle tute spaziali della NASA dagli anni ’60 fino al 2011, attraversando i principali programmi dell’esplorazione umana: Mercury, Gemini, Apollo e Space Shuttle.

Una piattaforma che raccoglie immagini e dati provenienti dalle missioni orbitali NASA.
Archivio digitale dedicato alla superficie di Marte e ai dati raccolti dai rover.
Una raccolta interattiva delle comunicazioni audio delle missioni Apollo.
Visualizzazione generativa dei segnali radio provenienti dalle missioni spaziali.
Archivio di fotografie, registrazioni e materiali storici delle missioni lunari.
Esperienza interattiva basata sugli esperimenti NASA relativi alla gravità.
Esplorazione interattiva di nebulose e galassie tramite immagini NASA.
Timeline interattiva dedicata ai centri di controllo NASA.
Visualizzazione 3D degli anelli di Saturno e della loro struttura.
Visualizzazione dati sui cambiamenti climatici osservati dai satelliti NASA.
Mappa interattiva delle principali missioni spaziali NASA.
Esperienza digitale sulle prime missioni spaziali umane.
Archivio immersivo di fotografie astronomiche storiche.
Esperienza educativa dedicata all’esplorazione di Marte.
Una raccolta visuale dedicata alle missioni Apollo.
Esplorazione interattiva di nebulose e sistemi stellari.
Visualizzazione delle orbite e dei movimenti planetari del Sistema Solare.
Archivio multimediale dedicato alle comunicazioni tra Terra e Luna.
Database visuale di missioni e satelliti NASA.
Esperienza sonora che traduce dati spaziali in paesaggi audio.
